data.title: Aptos Foundation Proposes Changes to Staking Rewards and Gas Fees
data.description: The Aptos Foundation has announced a proposed update to its tokenomics, aiming to adjust staking rewards and gas fees. According to NS3.AI, the foundation suggests reducing the annual percentage rate (APR) for staking rewards from 5.19% to 2.6% through a governance process. Additionally, it plans to increase gas fees by tenfold, with these fees to be paid in APT tokens, which will be completely burned. Furthermore, the foundation intends to implement a protocol-level hard cap of 2.1 billion APT tokens.

data.title: AI Leaders Avoid Interaction at India Summit
data.description: Sam Altman and Dario Amodei, the CEOs of OpenAI and Anthropic respectively, declined to hold hands during an AI summit in India. Bloomberg posted on X that the two executives, positioned next to each other among business and political leaders, chose to keep their hands raised and avoided making eye contact.

data.title: OpenAI CEO Highlights Uncertainty in Superintelligence Development
data.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman emphasized the need for humility regarding our understanding of superintelligence. Bloomberg posted on X, Altman expressed concerns about the potential misuse of this technology by authoritarian regimes during his address at India's AI summit. He highlighted the unpredictable nature of superintelligence and the importance of cautious development and deployment. Altman's remarks underscore the ongoing debate about the ethical and societal implications of advanced AI technologies.

data.title: South Korean prosecutors recover stolen Bitcoin worth $21.4 million
data.description: The Gwangju District Prosecutors' Office in South Korea has recovered approximately $21.4 million worth of Bitcoin stolen from its custody last year. The assets, originally seized from a gambling platform, were stolen in August after investigators mistakenly accessed a phishing website and leaked their mnemonic phrase. Because prosecutors blocked transactions in the relevant wallets, making it difficult to liquidate the assets, the hackers returned 320.8 Bitcoins to the official wallet on Tuesday. The recovered Bitcoins have been transferred to a local exchange, and the hackers' identities remain unknown.

data.title: Strategies for Navigating Unusual Market Conditions
data.description:
In the face of a peculiar market environment, investors are presented with three primary strategies: ignoring the fluctuations, actively trading, or succumbing to panic. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting these approaches as potential responses to the current market dynamics. 

Ignoring the market involves maintaining a long-term perspective and not reacting to short-term volatility. This strategy is often favored by investors who believe in the underlying strength of their investments and prefer to ride out temporary market disruptions.

Alternatively, some investors choose to actively trade, taking advantage of market swings to buy low and sell high. This approach requires a keen understanding of market trends and the ability to make quick decisions based on available data.

The third option, panicking, is generally considered the least advisable. Panic selling can lead to significant losses, as investors may sell assets at a low point, only to see the market recover shortly thereafter.

Each strategy carries its own risks and rewards, and the choice largely depends on an investor's risk tolerance, market knowledge, and investment goals. As the market continues to exhibit unusual behavior, investors are encouraged to carefully consider their options and choose a strategy that aligns with their financial objectives.

data.title: South Korea's Virtual Asset Tax Faces Potential Delay Due to Readiness Issues
data.description: South Korea's upcoming virtual asset tax, scheduled for implementation in 10 months, is encountering substantial administrative and legal challenges. According to NS3.AI, the necessary supporting statutes and detailed regulations have not been sufficiently developed in preparation for the tax's introduction. Experts indicate that if progress is not made, there is a possibility of a fourth postponement of the tax implementation.

data.title: U.S. Weekly Jobless Claims Expected to Show Slight Decrease
data.description: The number of initial jobless claims in the United States for the week ending February 14 is set to be released tonight at 21:30 UTC+8. According to BlockBeats, the previous figure stood at 227,000, while the forecast anticipates a slight decrease to 225,000.

data.title: Anthropic-Backed PAC Invests in AI Safety Advocate's Campaign
data.description: A political action committee supported by Anthropic is allocating $450,000 to support a congressional candidate in New York who advocates for safety regulations in artificial intelligence. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the financial backing aimed at promoting the candidate's stance on AI safety measures. This move underscores the growing interest and investment in political figures who prioritize regulatory frameworks for emerging technologies.

data.title: CVC Capital Partners Considers Sale of D-Marin with Goldman Sachs
data.description: CVC Capital Partners is reportedly collaborating with Goldman Sachs to evaluate the potential sale of D-Marin, a Mediterranean marina operator. Bloomberg posted on X, citing unnamed sources familiar with the matter. The private equity firm is exploring strategic options for D-Marin, which could include a sale, as part of its ongoing assessment of its investment portfolio. The discussions are still in the early stages, and there is no certainty that a transaction will occur. CVC Capital Partners and Goldman Sachs have not commented on the matter. D-Marin operates a network of marinas across the Mediterranean, providing services to yacht owners and maritime enthusiasts. The potential sale comes amid a broader trend of private equity firms reassessing their holdings in response to changing market conditions.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Russia's Potential Dollar Trade with US Could Impact Gold Prices
data.description: Russia is contemplating a return to dollar-based trade with the United States, a move that could challenge the BRICS bloc's current strategy of reducing reliance on the dollar by increasing gold reserves. According to NS3.AI, BRICS nations have collectively accumulated over 6,000 tonnes of gold, contributing to a rise in gold prices toward $7,000 per ounce. A potential trade agreement between Russia and the US could bolster the dollar, diminish the momentum of central banks' gold purchases, and lead to a notable decline in gold prices.

data.title: Injective's Price Surges Over 12% Following Governance Proposal Approval
data.description:
Injective (INJ) has seen a significant price increase of over 12% following the approval of the IIP-619 governance proposal, which enhances its MultiVM EVM architecture. According to NS3. AI, this development has coincided with Pineapple Financial (DAT) acquiring 7% of INJ's total supply, indicating a resurgence of institutional interest. Despite these positive trends and consistent on-chain activity, INJ's price remains more than 90% below its all-time high, as it continues to face challenges from the broader altcoin market sentiment
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/293290845353393?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: World Liberty Financial tokenizes revenue from the Trump Resort Maldives.
data.description: World Liberty Financial announced plans to tokenize loan proceeds related to the Trump International Hotel & Resort in the Maldives. The project is a collaboration between World Liberty Financial, luxury property developer DarGlobal PLC, and tokenization platform Securitize. Instead of providing direct ownership of the resort, the scheme allows investors to acquire loan proceeds related to the project's development financing. The investment is open to accredited investors compliant with Regulation D 506(c) and non-U.S. individuals compliant with Regulation S, and the tokens are expected to be issued on a public blockchain. The resort is scheduled for completion in 2030.

data.title: Nigerian President Signs Amendments to Electoral Law
data.description: Nigerian President Bola Tinubu has signed amendments to the nation's electoral law, a move that has sparked concerns among critics who fear it may undermine safeguards ahead of the presidential election next year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the apprehensions surrounding the changes. The amendments have been a topic of debate, with some arguing that they could impact the integrity of the electoral process. As Nigeria prepares for the upcoming election, the modifications to the law are expected to play a significant role in shaping the political landscape.

data.title: Injective's Price Surges Over 12% Following Governance Proposal Approval
data.description: Injective (INJ) has seen a significant price increase of over 12% following the approval of the IIP-619 governance proposal, which enhances its MultiVM EVM architecture. According to NS3.AI, this development has coincided with Pineapple Financial (DAT) acquiring 7% of INJ's total supply, indicating a resurgence of institutional interest. Despite these positive trends and consistent on-chain activity, INJ's price remains more than 90% below its all-time high, as it continues to face challenges from the broader altcoin market sentiment.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Trump Explores Alternative Tariff Strategies Amid Legal Challenges
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is considering alternative methods to impose tariffs if the Supreme Court rules against his use of a 1977 emergency law. Bloomberg posted on X that Trump is exploring other legal avenues to continue his trade policies. The 1977 law, which has been a cornerstone of Trump's tariff strategy, is under scrutiny for its validity in the current context. Legal experts suggest that Trump could leverage other legislative tools to maintain his tariff agenda, ensuring that his trade objectives are met despite potential judicial setbacks. The administration remains committed to its trade goals, seeking ways to navigate legal challenges while pursuing economic strategies that align with its broader policy framework.

data.title: Romeo Appointed as New Cabinet Secretary
data.description: Romeo, aged 51, has been appointed to succeed Chris Wormald as the Cabinet Secretary. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this significant change in the government leadership. The appointment marks a new chapter in the administration, with Romeo bringing his extensive experience to the role. The transition is expected to bring fresh perspectives to the Cabinet's operations, as Romeo steps into this pivotal position.

data.title: Severe Snowfall Causes Disruptions at Frankfurt Airport
data.description: Germany's largest airport, Frankfurt Airport, is experiencing significant disruptions due to heavy snowfall in the region. Bloomberg posted on X that the adverse weather conditions have led to numerous flight cancellations and delays, affecting both domestic and international travel. Airport authorities are working to clear runways and ensure passenger safety, but the severe weather continues to pose challenges. Travelers are advised to check with airlines for the latest updates on flight schedules. The situation remains fluid as efforts to manage the impact of the snowfall continue.

data.title: The United States has amassed its largest air power presence in the Middle East since 2003.
data.description: The U.S. air power buildup in the Middle East is the largest since the Iraq War began in 2003. The more than eight-year-long Iraq War started with the so-called "shock and awe" bombing campaign. Now, the U.S. is building up forces in the region as President Trump considers a strike against Iran. The U.S. has deployed dozens of aircraft and several aircraft carriers to the region. These fighter jets include F-16s, F-22s, and F-35s. This U.S. military buildup enables the U.S. to conduct a military operation that is longer than the B-2 bomber strikes against Iranian nuclear facilities during the Iraq War last June. (Jin Shi)
